changes.motivation: The docker image build (packages.image) was accidentally removed from the root flake.nix during earlier environment isolation refactoring work, even though it was unrelated to that feature.
changes.summary: Restored the packages.image dockerTools.streamLayeredImage definition in the root flake.nix.

changes.motivation: TypeScript did not recognize .nix file imports using { type: 'file' } because no module declaration existed for that file extension.
changes.summary: Added `declare module '*.nix'` to packages/cli/src/types/files-and-templates.d.ts so TypeScript accepts .nix file imports.

changes.motivation: An unused CLIError import was left in setupEnvironmentFiles.ts causing a TypeScript error.
changes.summary: Removed the unused CLIError import from packages/cli/src/commands/env/add/setupEnvironmentFiles.ts.

changes.motivation: The per-environment flake.nix template used by pf env add was overly complex (14 explicit nixpkgs inputs, manual package wiring) instead of matching the simple pattern used in packages/installer/install.sh.
changes.summary: Rewrote packages/cli/src/files/nix/flake.nix to use the installer's simple pattern: two inputs (flake-utils and panfactum), and a single devShell = panfactum.lib.${system}.mkDevShell { } output.

changes.motivation: The existing environment flake.nix files in environments/development, environments/management, environments/test, and environments/production were all using the same verbose 220-line pattern with manual nixpkgs input wiring, which should be replaced by the simple installer-compatible pattern.
changes.summary: Replaced all four environment flake.nix files with the simple 13-line pattern delegating to panfactum.lib.${system}.mkDevShell { }, matching the installer and the updated CLI template.

problems: The environment flake.nix files were generated with a complex 220-line pattern that duplicated all of the root flake's input wiring, instead of using the simple mkDevShell API already documented in packages/installer/install.sh. This created a significant discrepancy between what the installer sets up for new users and what pf env add generates, with no documentation explaining the intended approach.

The files-and-templates.d.ts type declaration file had module declarations for .hcl, .tf, .tftpl, .yaml, and *envrc files, but was missing a declaration for .nix files. Since .nix files were being imported the same way (with { type: 'file' }), the omission was a silent gap that caused a TypeScript error discoverable only at typecheck time.

The root flake exports lib.${system}.mkDevShell via flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem, but this API contract is not documented anywhere. The agent had to grep the root flake.nix and cross-reference the installer script to confirm the correct call site. A brief comment in the root flake explaining the exported lib API would prevent this discovery overhead.

When searching for packages/installer/install.sh, the agent searched only within the current git worktree and its remote tracking branches, failing to find the file. The file exists in the main worktree at a different path. There is no documented convention explaining that the worktree only contains a subset of the monorepo, causing repeated failed searches before the user directed the agent to search from the repo root.
